title: Tom Thumb
lines: Once a poor woodcutter and his wife wished for a child, even if it were no bigger than their thumb.
Soon a son was born to them, and though he was no larger than a thumb, they named him Tom Thumb.
Despite his small size, Tom Thumb was clever and brave.
One day his father was setting out to cut wood and said he wished Tom could help pull the cart.
'I will pull the cart, Father,' said Tom Thumb.
His father placed him in the horse's ear, and Tom Thumb drove the horse to the forest.
A man saw the horse and heard Tom Thumb speaking but could not see where the voice was coming from.
The man stole the horse, thinking to sell it and become rich.
Tom Thumb managed to escape and made his way home.
Another time, Tom Thumb was almost eaten by a cow and swallowed by a giant, but each time he escaped through his cleverness.
Once, he was swallowed by a wolf, but he tricked the wolf into jumping into a well and drowning.
Tom Thumb's bravery and wits kept him safe from all dangers.
In the end, his father became a rich man, and Tom Thumb lived a long and happy life.
